topic: minimax shrinking-window frontier for exact initiation-time causal survival effects: in the single-initiation illness-death model of Martinussen-Knudsen-Rytgaard arXiv:2605.31130, let the intervention window b_n around an interior initiation time u* shrink to zero; under a known s-Holder initiation-time response class, local initiation-density positivity, CAR censoring, bounded hazards, and explicit b_n-weighted cross-fitted nuisance-product conditions, prove the sharp minimax absolute-error rate n^(-s/(2s+1)) for the exact-time terminal-event risk with a local-polynomial one-step estimator based on the published EIF and a matching local-bump lower bound, then derive a uniform triangular-array Gaussian approximation and a computable worst-case-Holder-bias-aware confidence interval with honest coverage and optimal expected length; retain the 1,896-couple immediate-versus-six-month IUI treatment-delay analysis as the applied consumer

open_problems.open_problem: Does a unique s-Hölder initiation-time response version make exact-time terminal-event risk at an interior u* estimable at the sharp minimax absolute-error rate n^(-s/(2s+1)) under CAR censoring and local initiation positivity?

source_refs.claim: The exact-time initiation-risk estimand is not pathwise differentiable, so the paper replaces it by a regular smoothed-window intervention and derives that target's EIF and one-step estimator.
source_refs.author: Torben Martinussen, Mark Bech Knudsen, and Helene Rytgaard

source_refs.claim: The paper obtains Hölder-model minimax rates for a pointwise causal target using localized fuzzy hypotheses and a matching local-polynomial causal estimator, but not for censored initiation-time survival risk.
source_refs.author: Edward H. Kennedy, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, James M. Robins, and Larry Wasserman

source_refs.claim: The paper derives DML rates and minimax lower bounds as a continuous-exposure stochastic intervention concentrates, but its static exposure experiment does not cover illness-death initiation hazards.
source_refs.author: Kyle Schindl, Shuying Shen, and Edward H. Kennedy

source_refs.excerpt: Restate the target as the smoothed functional theta_h(P), or add an explicit version/regularity condition
the at-a-point target ... is NOT a well-defined functional ... without an explicit version/regularity condition

open_problems.why_unsolved: Martinussen-Knudsen-Rytgaard explicitly stop at a fixed smoothed window because exact-time risk is non-pathwise differentiable, while the prior pointwise-CATE review shows that a shrinking-neighborhood bias statement does not even select an observed-law point value without a version restriction. Hölder regularity can repair that well-posedness problem, but neither source supplies an upper/lower frontier for a CAR-censored illness-death law. The missing proof must jointly control localization bias, the diverging EIF variance, b_n-weighted cross-fitted nuisance products, and a least-favorable hazard perturbation that preserves CAR, bounded hazards, and local positivity.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A well-posedness lemma for the unique Hölder response version together with a local-polynomial one-step upper bound and a CAR-compatible local-bump lower bound matching at n^(-s/(2s+1)).

writing_exemplar.why: Its exact-time nonregularity-to-window-regularization arc leads directly to the shrinking-window minimax question and its IUI application supplies the intended empirical consumer.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: time-dependent initiation affects survival -> exact-time risk is non-pathwise differentiable -> fixed windows restore regularity -> determine the sharp shrinking-window frontier

method_exemplars.technique: Localized fuzzy-hypothesis testing lower bound paired with a local-polynomial higher-order influence-function estimator and matching risk analysis.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It provides matching causal pointwise lower- and upper-bound machinery under Hölder smoothness, which must be transplanted to censored illness-death martingales.

method_exemplars.technique: Track an increasingly concentrated stochastic intervention through its efficiency bound, DML remainder, and local minimax lower-bound experiment.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its concentration parameter has the same information-theoretic role as the inverse shrinking initiation window.

method_exemplars.technique: Kernel-localized orthogonal score with cross-fitting, a bandwidth-indexed variance calculation, and nuisance-product conditions at the local stochastic scale.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest econometric template for localization-aware DML at a continuous treatment index, although the desired score is the published survival EIF.

open_problems.open_problem: Which primitive thresholds on the shrinking-window EIF norm, b_n-weighted nuisance products, and localized score-class complexity are necessary and sufficient for uniform Gaussian inference on exact initiation-time survival risk?

source_refs.claim: The paper proves asymptotic inference for its regular smoothed-intervention estimator, not a uniform triangular array whose intervention window vanishes.
source_refs.author: Torben Martinussen, Mark Bech Knudsen, and Helene Rytgaard

source_refs.claim: The paper gives uniform Bahadur representations and bootstrap bands for nonparametric continuous-treatment response functions, addressing ordinary continuous-treatment localization rather than censored multistate scores.
source_refs.author: Chunrong Ai, Wei Huang, and Zheng Zhang

source_refs.claim: The paper establishes pointwise and uniform cross-fitted inference for treatment-specific survival curves under baseline treatment, not a shrinking initiation-time intervention.
source_refs.author: Ted Westling, Alex Luedtke, Peter B. Gilbert, and Marco Carone
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: The main open work is replacing Assumption ... with a primitive lower-bound construction
add the missing necessity channels as a formal iff frontier
the ONE genuine open gap is the boundary-scale plug-in remainder rate

open_problems.why_unsolved: Uniform theory exists separately for ordinary continuous-treatment smoothing and for baseline-treatment survival curves, but the shrinking initiation-window score is simultaneously a censoring-adjusted martingale functional, an estimated cross-fitted score, and a changing local empirical-process class with a diverging Riesz norm. The prior DML-frontier proposal assumed the active critical scale instead of deriving it, and the failed boundary-functional proposal shows why a regular Donsker/Hadamard remainder cannot simply be reused at a singular scale. What is missing is both an upper coupling from primitive learner and CAR-hazard conditions and class-preserving alternatives that show which norm, product-rate, entropy, or effective-count violations destroy uniform approximation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An iff frontier theorem giving a uniform Bahadur representation and Gaussian or multiplier-bootstrap coupling below explicit local-Riesz, weighted nuisance-product, entropy, and effective-count thresholds, with counterexamples beyond each threshold.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates initiation-time survival contrasts and supplies the fixed-window EIF whose limit theory must be strengthened.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: initiation timing defines a causal survival curve -> smoothing permits fixed-window inference -> shrinking windows create a triangular estimated-score class -> characterize exactly when uniform Gaussian inference survives

method_exemplars.technique: Nonasymptotic Gaussian coupling and anti-concentration bounds for suprema of changing, potentially non-Donsker empirical-process classes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Bandwidth-indexed survival EIFs form a triangular local class, so the coupling must track its growing envelope and complexity.

method_exemplars.technique: Uniform local-linear Bahadur expansion and weighted bootstrap for a continuous-treatment response process, including localization-bias control.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It handles the continuous localization index and simultaneous process approximation, though not censored multistate martingales.

method_exemplars.technique: Cross-fitted survival-EIF expansion uniformly over event time with machine-learned nuisance estimators and censoring adjustment.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the survival-process half of the desired coupling, to be combined with shrinking initiation-time localization.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the honest confidence-set length frontier for exact-time initiation risk, from bias-aware Gaussian intervals when n b_n diverges to non-Gaussian or nonshrinking behavior when n b_n stays bounded?

source_refs.claim: The paper reports Wald inference for fixed smoothed interventions, while explicitly separating that regular target from nonregular exact-time initiation risk.
source_refs.author: Torben Martinussen, Mark Bech Knudsen, and Helene Rytgaard

source_refs.claim: The paper constructs worst-case-bias-aware local-polynomial intervals and derives optimal bandwidth and efficiency comparisons over nonparametric smoothness classes.
source_refs.author: Timothy B. Armstrong and Michal Kolesár

source_refs.excerpt: Necessity of rare-learning inequalities and bounded-count diameter are open questions.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Armstrong-Kolesár solve worst-case smoothing bias and expected-length efficiency for ordinary local regression, but the initiation-time estimator has a censoring-adjusted estimated EIF and bandwidth-dependent nuisance drift. The anchor's Wald intervals cover a fixed smoothed intervention, not the exact-time target, and undersmoothing discards the rate-optimal bandwidth. Moreover, the prior weak-event proposal flags that bounded effective count can invalidate Gaussian studentization and force nonshrinking diameter. The unresolved task is therefore to combine a computable Hölder bias envelope with triangular-array score approximation in the n b_n→∞ regime and characterize the different limit experiment when local initiation counts remain bounded.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A regime theorem giving a computable worst-case-Hölder-bias-aware interval with uniform coverage and a matching optimal-expected-length lower bound when n b_n→∞, plus a Poisson-limit or nonshrinking-diameter characterization when n b_n=O(1).

writing_exemplar.why: Its fixed-window confidence intervals and IUI treatment-delay analysis make the inferential target mismatch concrete.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: fixed-window EIF yields Wald intervals -> exact-time risk differs by localization bias -> MSE-optimal shrinking leaves nonnegligible bias -> construct honest optimal-length inference across effective-count regimes

method_exemplars.technique: Compute worst-case smoothness-class bias, use bias-to-standard-error-dependent critical values, and optimize expected interval length over bandwidth and kernels.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the exact honesty and optimal-length argument needed for a pointwise Hölder target at an MSE-optimal bandwidth.

method_exemplars.technique: Uniform Edgeworth expansion and robust-bias-corrected Studentization for local-polynomial confidence intervals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies coverage-error control if a higher-order initiation-time bias term can be estimated rather than bounded.

method_exemplars.technique: Gaussian approximation and anti-concentration for maxima of triangular empirical processes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Uniform bias-aware coverage requires a coupling whose error is small relative to the interval's local stochastic radius.

open_problems.open_problem: When the initiation-time response is s-Hölder but the local initiation density is only beta-Hölder with beta<s, what is the sharp minimax rate for exact-time terminal-event risk, and which perturbation channel determines the deficient-regime exponent?

source_refs.excerpt: Sharpen the beta<alpha open-ended question by naming two candidate phase forms or perturbation channels
the deficient-regime same-class upper frontier remains open

open_problems.why_unsolved: The accepted continuous-dose run proves only a lower floor and explicitly leaves the beta<s density-smoothness phase unresolved because it is not known whether density regularity is a genuine information bound or an upper-proof artifact. Exact initiation time has the same continuous-index normalization issue, now coupled to censoring and multistate hazards, so local positivity alone does not determine whether rough initiation density creates an additional minimax channel. This is secondary to the topic's known-density-smoothness route but is the closest reviewer-prescribed extension of its oracle frontier.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A complete (s,beta) minimax phase-diagram theorem with an attaining estimator in every regime and matching response-only and coupled response-density lower-bound experiments.

writing_exemplar.why: It directly frames continuous-index causal response estimation and the unresolved role of treatment-density smoothness.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: continuous treatment effects require localization -> higher-order debiasing yields a smoothness-dependent upper rate -> density smoothness enters that rate -> decide whether and how it changes the exact-time survival frontier

method_exemplars.technique: Localized higher-order influence-function estimator with continuous-index density normalization and an explicit anisotropic remainder calculation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Initiation time is another continuous treatment index whose local normalization can couple response and density nuisance errors.

method_exemplars.technique: Fuzzy-hypothesis construction separating target-response smoothness from nuisance-interaction channels at a localized causal target.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The phase diagram needs distinct response-only and coupled density-response perturbations with controlled testing distance.

literature_map:
Martinussen-Knudsen-Rytgaard (2026, arXiv:2605.31130) identify exact initiation-time risk as non-pathwise differentiable and derive an EIF only after window smoothing.
Their bandwidth is part of the intervention, so their regular-target inference does not settle a shrinking-window triangular array.
Their IUI analysis uses 1,896 couples and compares immediate, six-month, and never-initiate treatment-delay strategies.
Kennedy-Ma-McHugh-Small (2017, JRSSB, doi:10.1111/rssb.12212) introduced kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment curve estimation.
Colangelo-Lee (arXiv:2004.03036) give kernel-localized cross-fitted DML and bandwidth-dependent nuisance conditions for continuous treatments.
Bonvini-Kennedy (arXiv:2207.11825) study smoothness-dependent dose-response rates but not this censored illness-death frontier.
Kennedy-Balakrishnan-Robins-Wasserman (2024, AoS, doi:10.1214/24-AOS2369) pair localized fuzzy-hypothesis lower bounds with a minimax causal estimator.
Schindl-Shen-Kennedy (arXiv:2409.11967) track efficiency, DML bias, and minimax bounds as a continuous-exposure intervention concentrates.
Westling-Luedtke-Gilbert-Carone (2024, JASA, doi:10.1080/01621459.2023.2205060) prove pointwise and uniform cross-fitted survival-curve inference for baseline treatment.
Ai-Huang-Zhang (arXiv:2602.01595) provide uniform Bahadur representations and bootstrap bands for ordinary continuous-treatment response functions.
Chernozhukov-Chetverikov-Kato (2014, AoS, doi:10.1214/14-AOS1230) provide Gaussian coupling for changing non-Donsker empirical-process classes.
Armstrong-Kolesár (2020, QE, doi:10.3982/QE1199) derive honest worst-case-bias-aware local-polynomial intervals and optimal-length comparisons.
Calonico-Cattaneo-Farrell (2022, Bernoulli, doi:10.3150/21-BEJ1445) give uniform coverage-error expansions for robust bias-corrected local-polynomial inference.
The core tension is that fixed-window semiparametric regularity and ordinary local-polynomial minimax theory exist separately, but not for a shrinking, censored illness-death EIF with learned nuisances.
The live substrate is their intersection: exact-time well-posedness, shrinking localization, CAR-aware lower bounds, primitive nuisance thresholds, and honest optimal-length inference.
prior_proposal_map:
No earlier proposal under qid stat_initiation_time_minimax_frontier exists; its active directory contained only state and pipeline initialization records.
Downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1 supplied §11 and angle4_v2 evidence for missing primitive local-Riesz, nuisance, entropy, and Gaussian-necessity conditions.
Downgraded stat_sa_cate_pointwise/v1 supplied the point-target well-posedness warning and a reviewer-prescribed version/regularity repair.
Accepted stat_dose_response_minimax/holder_anisotropic_converse supplied the continuous-index analogue and its explicitly open rough-density beta<s phase.
Active stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference supplied the effective-count Gaussian/honest-diameter analogue and its open bounded-count regime.
Failed stat_prte_ot_boundary_adaptation/v1 supplied the boundary-scale plug-in-remainder failure mode.
No prior Martinussen-Knudsen-Rytgaard, illness-death, IUI, or exact initiation-time proposal was found elsewhere in doc/research.
